notes: C#/.NET Kafka consumer service; uses OpenTelemetry .NET SDK (manual traces + auto-instrumentation via OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting)
Java gRPC service; auto-instrumented via OpenTelemetry Java Agent (-javaagent); generates traces and metrics
C#/.NET gRPC service; uses OpenTelemetry .NET SDK for distributed traces, metrics, and logs
Go gRPC service; uses go.opentelemetry.io/otel SDK for traces, metrics, and logs; also publishes to Kafka
C++ gRPC service; uses opentelemetry-cpp SDK (OPENTELEMETRY_CPP_VERSION set in .env) for traces and metrics
Ruby HTTP service; uses opentelemetry-ruby gem (opentelemetry-sdk, opentelemetry-instrumentation-sinatra) for traces
Feature flag config only (demo.flagd.json); no application code and no OTel SDK integration
Elixir/Phoenix web application (mix.exs); uses OpenTelemetry Elixir libraries (opentelemetry_exporter, opentelemetry_phoenix) for traces
Kotlin/JVM Kafka consumer; auto-instrumented via OpenTelemetry Java Agent; reads orders topic and emits risk assessment spans
TypeScript/Next.js BFF; uses @opentelemetry/sdk-web browser SDK for client-side traces and @opentelemetry/sdk-node for SSR traces
Envoy proxy configured in envoy.tmpl.yaml to route OTLP HTTP (/otlp-http/) from browser to otel-collector:4318; CORS open for OTLP
Grafana config (grafana.ini) and dashboard/datasource provisioning only; Grafana is a visualization backend consuming from Prometheus/Jaeger/OpenSearch
nginx serving static product images; no OTel SDK; nginx stub_status endpoint is scraped externally by OTel Collector nginx receiver
Jaeger config (config.yml) only; Jaeger is the distributed trace storage and query backend receiving OTLP gRPC from the collector
Apache Kafka broker Dockerfile only; message broker has no OTel SDK; OTel Collector may scrape broker metrics via JMX
Python HTTP service (app.py); serves OpenAI-compatible API for product Q&A; uses OpenTelemetry Python SDK (opentelemetry-sdk in requirements.txt)
Python/Locust load testing tool; uses opentelemetry-sdk to inject W3C trace context headers into synthetic HTTP requests
Dockerfile for OpenSearch data store only; OpenSearch is the log storage backend receiving data from the OTel Collector opensearch exporter
IS the OpenTelemetry Collector (Contrib v0.146.1); config defines OTLP gRPC/HTTP receivers, spanmetrics connector, and exporters to Jaeger/Prometheus/OpenSearch
Node.js gRPC service; OTel SDK explicitly initialised in opentelemetry.js using @opentelemetry/sdk-node and @opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node
PostgreSQL 17 Dockerfile and database init scripts only; relational database has no OTel SDK; OTel Collector scrapes DB metrics via postgresql receiver
Go gRPC service; uses go.opentelemetry.io/otel SDK for traces, metrics, and logs; serves product data from JSON files
Service in OTel Demo exposing product review data; instrumented with OTel SDK (language identifiable from src/product-reviews/ source files)
Prometheus config (prometheus-config.yaml) only; Prometheus is the metrics time-series backend receiving OTLP HTTP push from the collector
PHP HTTP service; uses opentelemetry-php library (open-telemetry/sdk) for distributed traces on shipping quote calculations
React Native mobile application; uses @opentelemetry/sdk-react-native (experimental) for client-side mobile traces and metrics
Python gRPC service; uses OpenTelemetry Python SDK (opentelemetry-sdk, opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-grpc) for traces and metrics
Rust HTTP service; uses opentelemetry and opentelemetry-otlp crates for distributed traces on shipping cost calculations
